chapter_title.en: Chapter 49 – The Found and the Lost Properties

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/112)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/112)
text: مَنْ وَجَدَ شَيْئاً فَهُوَ لَهُ فَلْيَتَمَتَّعْ بِهِ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَهُ طَالِبُهُ فَإِذَا جَاءَ طَالِبُهُ رَدَّهُ إِلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Ibrahim Bin Is’haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who finds anything, so it is for him. So let him enjoy with it until its seeker comes along. So when its seeker comes along, he should return it to him’.
en.sarwar: 10. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ibrahim ibn Ishaq from ‘Abd Allah ibn Hammad from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Whoever finds something it is for him to benefit thereby until the owner comes. He then must give it to the owner.” (Consult Fatwa also.)
